title: 10 best herbal capsules for bronchitis

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with bronchitis because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When inhaled, this substance reduces swelling in the airways and soothes irritated mucous membranes, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, licorice root extract has been shown to increase mucus clearance, helping to expel excess phlegm from the lungs and sinuses. As a result, herbal licorice capsules can provide effective relief from bronchitis symptoms such as coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Herbal licorice capsules contain triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and congestion in the airways, relieving symptoms of bronchitis.

Expectorant: Licorice root has natural expectorant properties that can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and relieve congestion and coughing associated with bronchitis.
Antioxidant: Herbal licorice capsules contain antioxidants that can help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate symptoms of bronchitis.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This saponin compound helps with bronchitis by reducing inflammation and suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby alleviating symptoms such as coughing and wheezing.

Liquiritigenin: This flavonoid is known for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe the airways, reduce inflammation, and protect the lungs from oxidative damage associated with bronchitis.

Licoricidin: This isoflavane is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, making it effective in loosening and clearing mucus from the airways, thereby relieving coughing and congestion associated with bronchitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to make capsules for bronchitis due to their high glycyrrhizin content, which provides anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used due to their bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that aid in bronchitis treatment.
Barks: The barks are used for their saponin content, which helps to break down mucus and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, providing relief from bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Obtain high-quality dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder from a reliable supplier for 1000 capsules.
Mix 250 grams of the root powder with 250 grams of cornstarch in a bowl to create a uniform blend.
Add 50 grams of gelatin to the mixture and stir well for 5 minutes to combine.
Use a capsule filling machine to fill 1000 empty gelatin capsules with approximately 250 milligrams of the blend per capsule.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower capsules helps with bronchitis because they contain a potent anti-inflammatory compound called ursolic acid, which has been shown to reduce inflammation in the lungs and ease respiratory congestion. The capsule's natural expectorant properties also help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier for the body to expel. Additionally, purple coneflower's antimicrobial properties may help combat underlying infections that contribute to bronchitis symptoms, promoting a faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Purple coneflower's antimicrobial properties can help combat bacterial infections associated with bronchitis, reducing inflammation and congestion in the lungs.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The herb's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce swelling and irritation in the airways, making breathing easier and alleviating coughing and wheezing symptoms.

Expectorant action: Purple coneflower's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to expel, which can provide relief from bronchitis symptoms such as chest tightness and congestion.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation and congestion associated with bronchitis.

Caffeic Acid Derivatives: As phenolic compounds, caffeic acid derivatives may exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to combat oxidative stress and inflammation in the respiratory tract.
Alkylphenol Glycosides: These compounds may possess antimicrobial properties, which could help combat bacterial infections contributing to bronchitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary source of Echinacea's medicinal properties, including alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives, which have anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting effects.
Leaves: The leaves contain phenolic acids and flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce bronchitis symptoms.
Flowers: The flowers are a rich source of alkylamides, which have immunomodulatory effects, helping to stimulate the immune system and reduce the severity of bronchitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves from the plant when in full bloom.
Dry the harvested material in a single layer at 40°c for 24 hours to remove moisture content.
Weigh 100g of dried echinacea purpurea and mix it with 100g of vegetable cellulose powder.
Use a capsule machine to fill 100 capsules with 0.5g of the echinacea purpurea mixture each.
Store the capsules in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight and heat sources.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme capsules helps with bronchitis because of its natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb thyme contains compounds that can help reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe for those suffering from bronchitis. Additionally, thyme's antimicrobial properties can combat the underlying infection causing the condition, helping to clear out excess mucus and promote a faster recovery. By taking herbal thyme capsules, individuals with bronchitis may experience relief from symptoms such as coughing, wheezing, and chest tightness.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial Properties: Thyme's antimicrobial properties can help combat bacterial infections that cause bronchitis, such as Streptococcus pneumoniae and Haemophilus influenzae.

Expectorant Properties: Thyme's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion and coughing fits associated with bronchitis.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling in the airways, which can alleviate symptoms such as chest tightness, wheezing, and shortness of breath common in bronchitis.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help combat bacterial infections in the lungs and reduce inflammation associated with bronchitis.

Carvacrol: Carvacrol has potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects that aid in fighting off respiratory pathogens, relieving congestion, and soothing the airways in individuals with bronchitis.
Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid possesses antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help protect the lungs from oxidative stress, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their rich content of thymol, a compound with antimicrobial and expectorant properties that help relieve bronchitis symptoms.
Buds: Utilized for their high concentration of essential oils, including thymol, which have decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties beneficial for bronchitis.
Stems: Employed for their secondary metabolites, including thymol and carvacrol, which exhibit antimicrobial and bronchodilatory effects that aid in bronchitis relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers when the plant is in full bloom and the foliage is dry.
Dry the harvested plant material in a clean environment away from direct sunlight for at least 2 weeks.
Crush 100g of dried thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powder with 500g of gelatin capsules in a ratio of 1:5 using a small bowl and spoon.
Fill the capsules with the herbal mixture and seal them immediately to preserve the potency of the herbal extract.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with bronchitis because they contain potent anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling in the airways, alleviating congestion and discomfort. The herb's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus, making it easier to breathe and cough up phlegm. Additionally, dandelion's antioxidant properties help combat oxidative stress and inflammation, further reducing bronchial irritation and promoting overall respiratory health. By addressing the root causes of bronchitis, these capsules can provide effective relief from symptoms and support the body's natural healing process.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Dandelion's antioxidant compounds, such as vitamins A and C, and its anti-inflammatory agents, like flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce inflammation in the airways, relieving bronchial congestion and discomfort.
Expectorant Properties: The mucilage present in dandelion root helps loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to expel and reducing coughing fits associated with bronchitis.

Antibacterial and Antiviral Properties: Dandelion's bioactive compounds have been shown to exhibit antibacterial and antiviral activity against various respiratory pathogens, which can help combat infections that exacerbate bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Taraxasterol: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and congestion in the airways associated with bronchitis.
Taraxinic acid: This sesquiterpene lactone has expectorant properties, which can help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe.

Flavonoids (e.g., taraxasterol acetate): These plant compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by bronchitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate bronchitis symptoms.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help soothe the respiratory tract.
Roots: Serve as a rich source of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that aid in relieving bronchitis symptoms and promoting mucus expulsion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers and dry them completely in a dark place for 7 days.
Grind the dried taraxacum officinale into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the taraxacum officinale powder with a binding agent like gum arabic in a 10 to 1 ratio.
Fill gelatin capsules with 400 milligrams of the taraxacum officinale mixture and seal them immediately.
Store the taraxacum officinale capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 12 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garlic capsules helps with bronchitis because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which help combat infection and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract. The active compounds in garlic, such as allicin and ajoene, have been shown to inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria and viruses that can exacerbate bronchitis symptoms. Additionally, garlic's natural expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and alleviate congestion. By targeting both infection and inflammation, herbal garlic capsules may provide relief from bronchitis symptoms and promote a faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-inflammatory Properties: Garlic capsules contain compounds like allicin, which helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the lungs, alleviating symptoms of bronchitis.

Expectorant and Mucolytic Properties: The sulfur compounds in garlic capsules can help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion associated with bronchitis.
Antimicrobial Properties: Garlic's antimicrobial properties can help combat bacterial and viral infections that contribute to bronchitis, reducing the severity of symptoms and promoting recovery.
remedy_constituents: Allicin: A sulfur-containing compound that breaks down into other active compounds, helping to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that can cause bronchitis.
Quercetin: A flavonoid antioxidant that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe.

S-allylcysteine (SAC): A stable and bioavailable form of allicin, which has been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to combat bronchitis-causing pathogens and reduce inflammation.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They contain compounds with antimicrobial and antiviral properties that help combat bronchitis-causing infections.
Buds: The buds contain a higher concentration of active compounds compared to mature leaves, making them more effective for bronchitis relief.
Leaves: The leaves are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and alleviate bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest allium sativum bulbs at the end of the growing season when the tops of the plants begin to yellow.
Dry the harvested allium sativum bulbs in a warm dry place for at least two weeks to reduce moisture content.
Grind dried allium sativum bulbs into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice grinder.
Mix 300 to 500 milligrams of the powdered allium sativum with a small amount of vegetable cellulose to form a uniform blend.
Fill gelatin capsules with the uniform allium sativum blend using a capsule filling machine or a small spoon.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mullein capsules helps with bronchitis because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated airways. The herb's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier for the body to expel. Additionally, mullein's antispasmodic effects relax constricted airway muscles, reducing coughing fits and congestion. By addressing these underlying issues, mullein capsules provide natural relief from bronchitis symptoms, allowing the body to heal and recover more effectively.
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Mullein's mucilage helps soothe and protect irritated mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with bronchitis.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in mullein capsules help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can exacerbate bronchial inflammation and damage caused by bronchitis.

Expectorant action: Mullein's expectorant properties help loosen and clear out mucus and congestion from the airways, making it easier to expel and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis such as coughing and wheezing.
remedy_constituents: Sesquiterpenes: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation and congestion in the airways associated with bronchitis.
Iridoid glycosides: These compounds exhibit expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving bronchitis symptoms.
Triterpenoids: These compounds have antimicrobial properties, which help combat infections that can cause bronchitis, reducing the severity and duration of the condition.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Verbascum thapsus are used to make capsules for bronchitis due to their expectorant properties, which help relieve coughs and congestion.
Seeds: Verbascum thapsus seeds are used to make capsules for bronchitis because they contain mucilages that soothe and calm the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and congestion.

Flowers: The flowers of Verbascum thapsus are used to make capsules for bronchitis due to their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help alleviate symptoms such as coughs, congestion, and sore throats.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of dried verbascum thapsus flowers from a trusted source or cultivate your own.
Dry the harvested flowers in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2-3 hours to preserve their potency.
Crush the dried flowers using a mortar and pestle to release their medicinal properties.
Mix 1 tablespoon of the crushed verbascum thapsus flowers with 2 tablespoons of gelatin in a small bowl.
Allow the mixture to set in a dehydrator at 150°f for 10-12 hours or until firm to create capsules.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense capsules helps with bronchitis because they contain boswellic acids, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation in the airways. This can help alleviate congestion and coughing associated with bronchitis. Additionally, frankincense has been shown to improve lung function by increasing airflow and reducing bronchial muscle spasms. The natural antiseptic properties of frankincense also help combat bacterial infections that often exacerbate bronchitis, promoting a healthy respiratory system and relieving symptoms such as wheezing and chest tightness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Frankincense contains boswellic acids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation in the airways, alleviating symptoms such as coughing and wheezing associated with bronchitis.

Antimicrobial: The active compounds in frankincense capsules, including boswellic acids and terpenes, have antibacterial properties that help combat infections caused by bacteria like Streptococcus pneumoniae, a common cause of bronchitis.
Mucolytic: Frankincense contains mucilages that help break down and dissolve mucus in the lungs, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion and coughing associated with bronchitis.
remedy_constituents: Acetyl-11-keto-β-boswellic acid (AKBA): This triterpenoid compound helps reduce inflammation in the airways, which can alleviate symptoms of bronchitis.
β-Boswellic acids (BAs): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the lungs associated with bronchitis.

Terpenoids (including boswellic acids): These compounds have anti-inflammatory, anti-oxidant, and bronchodilatory effects, which can help relax airway muscles, reduce inflammation, and improve breathing in individuals with bronchitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain the highest concentration of boswellic acids, which are responsible for the medicinal properties of Boswellia serrata.
Rhyzomes: They are another primary source of boswellic acids and are often used in combination with roots to enhance their efficacy.
remedy_recipe: Collect 500 grams of dried boswellia serrata bark and sift it through a 40-mesh sieve to ensure uniform particle size.
Grind the sifted boswellia serrata bark into a fine powder using a grinder or mill for 10 minutes.
Mix 10 grams of the ground boswellia serrata powder with 5 grams of starch powder and 2 grams of cellulose powder.
Fill 1000 empty gelatin capsules with the mixture from step 3 using a powder filler or capsule filler for 10 minutes.
Seal the filled capsules using a capsule sealer or a heat sealer at 150 degrees celsius for 5 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea capsules helps with bronchitis because they provide a concentrated and easy-to-absorb dose of natural ingredients that soothe and calm the respiratory system. The herbal extracts in these capsules work to reduce inflammation, loosen mucus, and ease coughs, providing fast and effective relief from bronchitis symptoms. By targeting the underlying causes of bronchitis, such as congestion and irritation, tea capsules help to alleviate discomfort and promote healing, allowing for a faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Eucalyptus: Eases congestion and opens airways by releasing menthol, which helps to thin mucus and relieve coughs.
Ginger: Soothes inflammation and reduces irritation in the throat and lungs, making it easier to breathe and alleviating discomfort.
Thyme: Has antibacterial properties that help combat infections caused by bronchitis, reducing the severity of symptoms and promoting healing.
remedy_constituents: Catechins (Epigallocatechin gallate, EGCG): EGCG has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and congestion in the airways associated with bronchitis.
Flavonoids (Quercetin): Quercetin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs, thus alleviating bronchitis symptoms.
Theaflavins: Theaflavins have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help protect the lungs from oxidative damage and inflammation, thus providing relief from bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part of Camellia sinensis for making tea, which is often used to help alleviate bronchitis symptoms due to its expectorant properties.
Stems: Stems from Camellia sinensis can be used to make capsules for bronchitis as they contain flavonoids and other compounds that help reduce inflammation and improve respiratory function.
Roots: Roots of Camellia sinensis are used in some herbal remedies for bronchitis due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help soothe and protect the respiratory tract.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 cup of dried camellia sinensis leaves and store them in an airtight container.
Grind 1 tablespoon of the dried leaves into fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix 1 teaspoon of the ground powder with 1 tablespoon of gelatin powder in a small bowl.
Heat 2 tablespoons of water in a double boiler and add the gelatin mixture stirring constantly for 5 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and harden then fill a capsule machine with the hardened gelatin mixture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint capsules helps with bronchitis because they contain menthol, a natural decongestant that soothes inflamed airways and eases congestion. The capsules' anti-inflammatory properties also reduce swelling in the lungs and bronchi, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, peppermint's calming effects can help alleviate coughing fits and promote relaxation, allowing the body to recover more effectively from an attack of bronchitis. As a result, herbal peppermint capsules provide natural relief from bronchitis symptoms, promoting overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Expectorant property: Peppermint capsules can help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up and breathe more easily, which is beneficial for relieving congestion associated with bronchitis.

Anti-inflammatory property: The menthol in peppermint has anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the lungs and airways, which can alleviate symptoms such as chest tightness and wheezing commonly experienced during bronchitis.

Antiviral property: Peppermint contains compounds that have antiviral properties, which can help combat the viral infection that causes bronchitis by inhibiting its growth and replication, thereby reducing the severity and duration of the illness.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis.
Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help thin mucus and make it easier to cough up, thereby relieving bronchitis symptoms.
Piperitenone: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help reduce inflammation and combat infections that can cause bronchitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their expectorant properties to help relieve bronchitis symptoms.
Stems: Contain menthol, which helps thin mucus and ease congestion.
Roots: Rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in soothing bronchitis.
remedy_recipe: Dry 500g of fresh mentha x piperita leaves in a food dehydrator at 40°c for 12 hours.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a spice grinder.
Mix 250g of the powdered mentha x piperita with 250g of gelatin to create a uniform blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500mg of the mentha x piperita-gelatin mixture using a powder filling machine.
Label and store the capsules in a cool dry place to preserve their potency and shelf life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenrod capsules helps with bronchitis because they contain a concentrated extract of Echinacea angustifolia, which has been traditionally used to soothe and calm respiratory issues. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling in the airways, making it easier for mucus to be cleared out of the lungs. Additionally, goldenrod capsules have been shown to increase the production of white blood cells, which helps fight off infections that can exacerbate bronchitis symptoms such as coughing and wheezing. By reducing inflammation and supporting immune function, goldenrod capsules can provide natural relief from bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Goldenrod's flavonoids and phenolic acids have been shown to reduce inflammation in the airways, which can help relieve congestion, coughing, and chest tightness associated with bronchitis.

Antimicrobial properties: The plant's compounds, particularly the sesquiterpenes, have been found to exhibit antimicrobial activity against various bacteria that can cause bronchitis, such as Streptococcus pneumoniae and Haemophilus influenzae.

Expectorant properties: Goldenrod's mucilages and saponins help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up and relieve congestion, which is a primary symptom of bronchitis.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids, particularly quercetin, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the airways, thereby alleviating bronchitis symptoms.

Sesquiterpene lactones: Sesquiterpene lactones, such as virgaurein and isovergaurein, have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and modulate the immune response in bronchitis.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, including ferulic acid, have been found to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help combat infections and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, thereby alleviating bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe bronchial irritation.
Stems: Used because they contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can aid in reducing bronchitis symptoms.
Roots: Used due to their rich content of phenolic acids and saponins, which can help reduce inflammation and fight infections in the respiratory tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the solidago virgaurea roots and stems when they are fresh and in full bloom.
Dry the harvested material in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried solidago virgaurea into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 1 part of the ground material with 2 parts of a binding agent such as gum arabic.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the powdered solidago virgaurea mixture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage capsules helps with bronchitis because they contain compounds that have natural anti-inflammatory properties. When taken regularly, sage capsules can help reduce inflammation in the airways and ease congestion, making it easier to breathe. The herb's expectorant properties also help loosen mucus, allowing for a more productive cough and faster healing of the lungs. Additionally, sage's antioxidants may help protect the lungs from further damage and promote overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Sage capsules contain salvene, a compound that reduces inflammation in the airways, which helps to relieve the coughing and wheezing associated with bronchitis.

Antimicrobial: The essential oils present in sage capsules, such as camphor and thujone, have antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial infections like bronchitis, reducing the severity of symptoms and promoting recovery.

Expectorant: Sage's saponins and volatile oils stimulate the production of mucus, making it easier to cough up phlegm and other debris from the lungs, which helps to clear out infected airways and reduce congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making it effective in reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract.
Bark: Contains bioactive flavonoids that help to soothe and calm the bronchi, reducing coughing and congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried salvia officinalis leaves from reputable sources ensuring potency and quality.
Crush 20 grams of dried leaves into fine powder using mortar and pestle within 30 minutes.
Mix the powdered herb with 100 ml of boiling water for 10-15 minutes to create a decoction.
Strain the decoction through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove solids and yield 50 ml of liquid extract within 20 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of powdered herb per capsule using a digital scale and airtight storage container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum capsules helps with bronchitis because they contain eucalyptus oil, which has natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The capsules help to ease congestion in the airways, reducing the production of mucus and making it easier to breathe. Additionally, the capsules have antispasmodic properties that can help to relax the muscles in the bronchial tubes, further relieving symptoms such as wheezing and coughing. As a result, Tasmanian blue gum capsules provide effective natural relief from the symptoms of bronchitis.
remedy_properties: Decongestant: Tasmanian Blue Gum's active compound, eucalyptol, helps to clear mucus and reduce congestion in the airways, relieving symptoms of bronchitis.
Antitussive: The herb's expectorant properties help to break down and remove thick mucus, reducing the frequency and severity of coughing fits associated with bronchitis.
Anti-inflammatory: Tasmanian Blue Gum's polyphenolic compounds have been shown to reduce inflammation in the airways, which can exacerbate symptoms of bronchitis and slow recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and terpenoids, which help to alleviate coughs and congestion associated with bronchitis.
Barks: The barks of the Tasmanian blue gum plant have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, including bronchitis, due to their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried eucalyptus globulus leaves in a clean environment to ensure potency and purity.
Grind 250 grams of the dried eucalyptus globulus leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the ground eucalyptus globulus powder with 125 milliliters of distilled water in a double boiler to create a decoction that simmers for 30 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth into a clean container, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid extract for further processing.
Fill 500-milligram vegetarian capsules with the eucalyptus globulus extract and store them in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain capsules helps with bronchitis because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe irritated airways and reduce inflammation, alleviating congestion and coughing. The plant's mucilages also help to coat and protect the respiratory tract, making it easier for mucus to be expelled from the lungs. Additionally, plantain's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out excess mucus, making it easier to breathe and reducing the severity of bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Plantain capsules contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been shown to reduce inflammation in the airways, alleviating symptoms of bronchitis such as coughing and shortness of breath.
Mucilages: The mucilage content in Plantain capsules helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with bronchitis.
Expectorant properties: The saponins present in Plantain capsules have been found to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and relieve congestion caused by bronchitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Plantain roots are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective at reducing inflammation and soothing respiratory tract irritations.
Barks: The bark of the plantain plant contains antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to protect against oxidative stress and alleviate bronchial spasms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh plantago major leaves in the morning when they are at their most potent.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm and well-ventilated area for two weeks.
Grind dried plantago major leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 400mg of ground plantago major with 200mg of lactose and fill gelatin capsules immediately.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fenugreek capsules helps with bronchitis because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains compounds that reduce swelling in the airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, fenugreek's expectorant qualities help loosen and clear out mucus, allowing for a more efficient clearance of phlegm from the lungs. As a result, symptoms such as coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath are alleviated, providing relief to those suffering from bronchitis.
remedy_properties: Expectorant property: Fenugreek's expectorant property helps to loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion symptoms associated with bronchitis.

Anti-inflammatory property: Fenugreek's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce inflammation in the airways, which can exacerbate bronchitis symptoms such as wheezing, coughing, and chest tightness.
Antimicrobial property: Fenugreek's antimicrobial property helps to combat bacterial infections that can cause or complicate bronchitis, reducing the risk of complications and promoting recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fenugreek roots have been traditionally used to soothe and calm coughs, making them a popular choice for bronchitis treatment.
Seeds: The seeds of fenugreek contain mucilages that help to reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, relieving symptoms of bronchitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest trigonella foenum graecum leaves in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the harvested leaves using shade drying or machine drying for 24 hours.
Grind 20g of dried trigonella foenum graecum leaves into a fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered herb with 40g of magnesium stearate and 20g of gelatin in a bowl.
Fill small capsules with 300mg of the herbal mixture and seal with cotton thread for 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel capsules helps with bronchitis because of their natural expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear mucus from the airways. The herb's anti-inflammatory compounds also reduce swelling in the bronchial tubes, allowing for easier breathing. Additionally, fennel's antispasmodic effects can help relax the smooth muscles surrounding the airways, reducing coughing fits and wheezing. By promoting healthy respiratory function, herbal fennel capsules provide a natural remedy to alleviate bronchitis symptoms and promote recovery.
remedy_properties: Expectorant property: Fennel's expectorant nature helps to loosen and clear out mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up and breathe more easily.
Anti-inflammatory property: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the lungs and bronchial tubes, which can alleviate symptoms such as wheezing and shortness of breath.
Antioxidant property: Fennel's antioxidants may help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and congestion associated with bronchitis.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain a compound called anethole, which has been shown to help relax airway muscles and reduce coughing spasms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried foeniculum vulgare seeds and grind them into a fine powder using an electric grinder.
Mix 200mg of ground foeniculum vulgare seeds with 400mg of tapioca starch in a small bowl.
Add 10ml of distilled water to the mixture and stir until it forms a smooth paste.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with 500mg of the foeniculum vulgare seed paste and seal them immediately.
Store the finished capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with bronchitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The curcumin in turmeric reduces inflammation in the airways, making it easier for patients to breathe and alleviating symptoms such as coughing and wheezing. Additionally, turmeric's antimicrobial properties help combat infections that can exacerbate bronchitis. By reducing swelling and fighting off infection, turmeric capsules can provide natural relief from bronchitis symptoms, helping patients recover faster and more effectively.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric's active compound, curcumin, reduces inflammation in the airways, which can help alleviate symptoms such as coughing and wheezing associated with bronchitis.
Antimicrobial: Curcumin has been shown to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria that can cause bronchitis, such as Streptococcus pneumoniae, helping to prevent infection and promote healing.
Expectorant: Turmeric's mucilages help loosen and expel mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion associated with bronchitis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of turmeric contain a high concentration of curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that helps alleviate symptoms of bronchitis such as coughing and congestion.
Roots: Turmeric roots are used to make medicinal capsules for bronchitis due to their rich antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation in the airways and ease breathing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried turmeric powder from 3 grams of fresh rhizome curcuma longa for each 500mg capsule.
Mix 250mg of harvested turmeric powder with 250mg of gum acacia in a small bowl until well combined.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them tightly to ensure proper dosing.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or refrigerate for longer use.
Take one capsule twice daily, 30 minutes before meals, and drink plenty of water to enhance bioavailability.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm capsules helps with bronchitis because it contains rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling in the airways. This can provide relief from the congestion and coughing associated with bronchitis. Additionally, lemon balm's calming effects on the respiratory system can help to ease shortness of breath and promote easier breathing. Its natural expectorant properties can also help to loosen and clear out mucus, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antitussive properties: Lemon balm has been shown to reduce cough frequency and severity, providing relief from persistent coughing associated with bronchitis.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The flavonoids present in lemon balm have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis such as wheezing and shortness of breath.
Expectorant properties: Lemon balm's expectorant action can help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion associated with bronchitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the lungs, providing relief from bronchial congestion.

Leaves: The leaves of the lemon balm plant contain a compound called rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to have antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to relieve coughs and wheezing associated with bronchitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality melissa officinalis leaves and flowers at peak bloom.
Dry the plant material using a food dehydrator for 12 hours at 60 degrees fahrenheit.
Grind the dried melissa officinalis into a fine powder using an electric spice grinder.
Mix one part powdered melissa officinalis with two parts excipient such as silica gel or potato starch to create a blend.
Fill empty capsules with 500 milligrams of the blend and seal immediately for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder capsules helps with bronchitis because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling in the airways, making it easier to breathe. The capsular form of elderberry allows for easy absorption into the bloodstream, providing rapid relief from bronchial constriction and coughing fits. Additionally, elder capsules possess antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial and viral infections that can exacerbate bronchitis symptoms, promoting a speedy recovery.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Elderberry's antiviral compounds, such as anthocyanins and flavonoids, help combat the viral infection that causes bronchitis, reducing its severity and duration.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The bioactive compounds in elder capsules, like quercetin and kaempferol, have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and congestion in the airways associated with bronchitis.
Mucolytic properties: Elderberry's mucolytic agents, such as phenolic acids and terpenes, break down thick mucus, making it easier to expel and reducing the burden on the lungs and respiratory tract.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elder roots are used to make capsules because they have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe.

Rhyzomes: Elder rhyzomes are used to make capsules because they contain bioactive compounds that exhibit antitussive (cough-suppressing) and expectorant properties, helping to relieve coughs and congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried sambucus nigra flowers and stems from a reputable source measuring exactly 50 grams.
Grind the dried material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 20 grams of the powdered sambucus nigra with 30 grams of silica gel in a bowl allowing to sit for 24 hours.
Fill empty #00 capsules with the mixture weighing exactly 250 milligrams and seal tightly.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for bronchitis are natural supplements that contain a blend of herbs, botanicals, and plant extracts that help alleviate symptoms of bronchitis. These capsules work by soothing inflammation in the airways, reducing coughing, and opening up air passages to improve breathing. Examples of herbal capsules that can help with bronchitis include Echinacea, Goldenseal, and Thyme. By using these capsules, individuals suffering from bronchitis may experience relief from wheezing, chest tightness, and fatigue, allowing them to breathe easier and enjoy a better quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to bronchitis that are treated with herbal capsules are chronic coughs, sinusitis, and laryngitis. Herbal formulas containing ingredients like slippery elm, licorice root, and marshmallow root can help soothe irritated respiratory passages and reduce inflammation. Capsules may also contain expectorants like mullein and thyme to loosen mucus and promote easier breathing. These natural remedies can provide relief from congestion, coughing, and chest tightness associated with these conditions.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with bronchitis is a blend of Echinacea, Ginger, and Eucalyptus. Echinacea supports the immune system, reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. Ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe a sore throat and calm coughs. Eucalyptus oil has decongestant properties, relieving congestion and opening airways. These three herbs work together to alleviate bronchitis symptoms, promoting faster recovery and reducing the risk of complications. They can be taken in capsule form, under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by The Korean journal of internal medicine, the herbal capsules HL301, which is a combined product of seven medicinal plants, were found to be effective in treating acute bronchitis. In a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, multicenter trial, 166 patients with acute bronchitis received either a placebo or HL301 (600 mg/day) for 7 days, and the results showed that the patients treated with HL301 had a significant improvement in their bronchitis severity score compared to the placebo group, with a change of 4.57 ± 1.82 in the HL301 group versus 3.15 ± 3.08 in the placebo group (p < 0.01). This suggests that herbal capsules like HL301 can be a good option for treating acute bronchitis due to their effectiveness in reducing symptoms and improving quality of life.
